channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: Fully Funded PhD Scholarships at UQ: Co-Develop Solutions to Climate-Driven Loss and Damage in the Pacific
item.dc:creator: Maria Ramirez
item.description:
The University of Queensland (UQ) is offering four fully funded PhD scholarships as part of the ARC Industry Laureate Fellowship, focused on co-developing solutions to climate-driven loss and damage in the Pacific. This is a unique opportunity to work closely with communities, governments, and regional partners to address the social, cultural, and justice dimensions of […]

The post Fully Funded PhD Scholarships at UQ: Co-Develop Solutions to Climate-Driven Loss and Damage in the Pacific appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The University of Queensland (UQ) is offering four fully funded PhD scholarships as part of the ARC Industry Laureate Fellowship, focused on co-developing solutions to climate-driven loss and damage in the Pacific. This is a unique opportunity to work closely with communities, governments, and regional partners to address the social, cultural, and justice dimensions of climate change.

These scholarships are ideal for candidates passionate about applied research and committed to creating meaningful change for Pacific Island communities.

Why Apply?

PhD candidates will engage in research that directly impacts communities in the Cook Islands, Fiji, and Vanuatu, offering a rare combination of academic, field, and policy experience. Key benefits include:

  • Fully funded scholarship covering tuition fees, living stipend (AU$37,500 per annum, tax-free), and Overseas Student Health Cover
  • Access to world-class facilities and a dynamic, interdisciplinary research environment at the School of the Environment
  • Funded fieldwork and collaboration with NGOs, regional organizations, and government agencies
  • Mentorship from leading experts, including Professor Karen McNamara, Principal Supervisor

Research Focus

PhD projects will be co-designed with your advisory team to align with the Fellowship’s objectives:

  1. Develop and adapt methods for assessing climate-driven loss and damage
  2. Document lived experiences and community-led responses
  3. Implement and evaluate locally grounded interventions
  4. Inform policy and practice through multi-scalar insights and best-practice guidelines

Projects are flexible and tailored to your research interests, disciplinary background, and the needs of partner communities.

Who Should Apply

Ideal candidates will have academic achievement in:

  • Geography
  • Environmental science
  • Indigenous studies
  • Development studies or related fields

Experience or interest in climate adaptation, participatory research, or policy engagement is highly desirable. Scholars from Pacific Island nations, particularly Cook Islands, Fiji, and Vanuatu, are encouraged to apply.

How to Submit an Expression of Interest (EOI)

Submitting an EOI is the first step toward securing your PhD scholarship. Here’s what you need to know:

Step 1: Prepare Your EOI

Include the following details:

  • Proposed supervisor
  • Scholarship details
  • Previous academic qualifications
  • Referee contacts (minimum two)
  • English language proficiency information

Step 2: Upload Required Documents

  • Academic curriculum vitae (CV)
  • Academic transcripts and degree certificates
  • English language documents (if applicable)

Formatting Guidelines:

  • PDF format only
  • File name: LASTNAME_firstname_document-name.pdf
  • Include both original and official English translations if documents are in another language

Step 3: Academic CV

Your CV should focus on academic and research achievements, including:

  • Personal details (name, contact, nationality, languages, ORCID ID)
  • Education and academic awards
  • Employment history and research experience
  • Research outputs, publications, and grants
  • Optional: a 200-word contextual statement on achievements relative to opportunities

Do not include photographs, hobbies, or unrelated personal details.

Step 4: Referee Details

Provide at least two referees who can speak to your academic or research abilities. UQ will contact referees directly; do not submit pre-written letters.

After Submitting Your EOI

  • Your nominated supervisor will review your EOI (response typically within two weeks).
  • If the supervisor is interested, an informal interview may be arranged to discuss your research fit.
  • If supported, you will be invited to submit a final application, including:
    • Passport copy
    • Additional documents (proof of citizenship, name change, previous research studies)
    • UQ-approved agent submission for some international applicants

If your EOI is not supported, you may choose another supervisor and submit a new EOI.

Deadline

All EOIs must be submitted by 15 April, 2026. Early submission is strongly recommended.

How to Apply

To start your application, visit the official UQ project page: UQ PhD Projects – Climate-Driven Loss and Damage

APPLY HERE. 

For more information about this apportunity, click here. 

Discover more scholarship opportunities, click here. 

Deadline: 15 April, 2026

For questions about the suitability of the project, contact Professor Karen McNamara at karen.mcnamara@uq.edu.au. Submit your expression of interest (EOI) via UQ PhD Projects.

The post Fully Funded PhD Scholarships at UQ: Co-Develop Solutions to Climate-Driven Loss and Damage in the Pacific appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: EU Call for Proposals in Ethiopia 2026: Funding for Civil Society and Global Gateway Action
item.dc:creator: Maria Ramirez
item.description:
A Major EU Funding Opportunity for Civil Society Organisations in Ethiopia The Delegation of the European Union to Ethiopia has opened a significant new funding opportunity for civil society organisations through the call titled “Supporting CSO role in sustainable and inclusive development and in Global Gateway Investments.” This initiative is designed to strengthen Ethiopian civil […]

The post EU Call for Proposals in Ethiopia 2026: Funding for Civil Society and Global Gateway Action appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
A Major EU Funding Opportunity for Civil Society Organisations in Ethiopia

The Delegation of the European Union to Ethiopia has opened a significant new funding opportunity for civil society organisations through the call titled “Supporting CSO role in sustainable and inclusive development and in Global Gateway Investments.” This initiative is designed to strengthen Ethiopian civil society organisations as independent, resilient, and accountable actors that can contribute to inclusive governance, equitable socio-economic development, and the implementation of Global Gateway investments in the country.

This call is part of the CSOs Thematic Programme 2021–2027 and builds on earlier EU support to civil society in Ethiopia. The programme reflects the EU’s broader commitment to democratic participation, rights-based development, and stronger civic engagement, while also linking civil society action to priority investment sectors under the Global Gateway strategy.

What This EU Call for Proposals Supports

The overall objective of the call is to strengthen Ethiopian civil society organisations so they can play a stronger role in inclusive governance and sustainable development, while also contributing to Global Gateway investments. The call is divided into two separate lots, and applicants may apply under one lot only. For either lot, proposals are expected to use an integrated approach and address at least three priority areas under the selected lot rather than presenting isolated activities.

This means applicants need to design proposals that are coherent, strategic, and clearly linked to measurable results. The guidelines also stress the importance of strong intervention logic, relevant indicators, monitoring plans, and meaningful partnerships between consortium members.

Funding Available: Budget, Lots, and Grant Size

The total indicative budget available under this call is EUR 5,829,000. The funding is split equally across the two lots, with EUR 2,914,500 allocated to Lot 1 and EUR 2,914,500 allocated to Lot 2. The guidelines note that the Lot 2 allocation is tied to the EU budget for 2027 and is therefore subject to the prior availability of those funds.

For both lots, the grant size is fixed at EUR 2,914,500, meaning this is a high-value funding opportunity aimed at large-scale actions. The EU contribution must represent 95% of the total eligible costs of the action, with the remaining balance financed from other sources unless the applicant can justify full financing as essential for implementation.

Lot 1: Strengthening Civil Society Capacity and Civic Space

Lot 1 focuses on strengthening civil society’s role, capacity, and enabling environment in Ethiopia. Its specific objective is to improve the capacity, sustainability, and influence of local and grassroots CSOs so they can engage more effectively in policy-making, advocacy, dialogue with authorities, monitoring, and accountability processes at both local and national levels.

Under this lot, priority areas include improving CSOs’ ability to influence public policy, support evidence-based advocacy, promote transparency and accountability, and build stronger partnerships and coalitions. It also supports inclusive social and economic development initiatives, stronger community engagement, and better internal governance, sustainability, fundraising, and organisational effectiveness. Another major focus is the protection of civic space and the promotion of an enabling legal and policy environment for civil society.

The call also places strong emphasis on Financial Support to Third Parties (FSTP) under Lot 1. Applicants are expected to establish and manage at least one open and competitive support mechanism for local and grassroots CSOs, including capacity building, mentoring, and tailored technical support. At least EUR 1,860,000 must be reserved for FSTP to local and grassroots CSOs, and at least EUR 291,000 must be allocated to strengthening the capacities of those recipients.

Lot 2: Civil Society Engagement in Global Gateway Priority Sectors

Lot 2 is aimed at strengthening civil society engagement in Global Gateway priority sectors in Ethiopia. Its objective is to enhance the role and capacity of CSOs to contribute to transparent, inclusive, and sustainable policy-making, reform processes, and investment processes in four sectors: clean energy transition, sustainable agri-food value chains, health systems strengthening, and human-centric digital transformation.

Priority areas under Lot 2 include improving governance and regulatory frameworks, increasing transparency and accountability in investment processes, empowering communities to participate in decision-making, and supporting inclusive skills development and economic opportunities linked to investment sectors. The call encourages actions that strengthen public oversight, promote environmental and social safeguards, support grievance mechanisms, and expand the role of communities and local CSOs across the investment cycle.

Actions under Lot 2 must be implemented in at least three specific locations in Ethiopia where Global Gateway projects are already being implemented, and applicants must clearly explain how their chosen areas connect to relevant Global Gateway investments.

Who Is Eligible to Apply?

This opportunity is intended for non-profit legal entities such as civil society organisations, including NGOs, networks, and associations. To qualify, the lead applicant must be a legal person, be non-profit-making, and be directly responsible for the preparation and management of the action. Eligible lead applicants must be effectively established in an EU Member State, Ethiopia, or another country eligible under Article 28 of the NDICI-Global Europe regulation.

A key requirement is that the lead applicant must apply together with at least two co-applicants established and registered in Ethiopia. In addition, the consortium must demonstrate experience managing at least one EU grant above EUR 1.5 million in the past three years, at least three years of practical experience working with local CSOs and building their capacity, and a proven track record in managing Financial Support to Third Parties schemes.

For lead applicants not established in Ethiopia, the rules are stricter. They must demonstrate prior knowledge of Ethiopia’s civil society landscape, show that they have implemented at least two projects with local CSOs, and prove that local CSOs have been involved in the conception, governance, implementation, monitoring, and reporting of the proposed action.

Project Duration, Locations, and Design Requirements

For both lots, the initial planned duration of an action must be at least 36 months and no more than 48 months.

For Lot 1, actions must take place in at least three locations from the following exclusive list: Somali, Harari, Sidama, Gambella, South West Ethiopia, and Dire Dawa City Administration.

Beyond location and duration, the guidelines require all proposals to demonstrate strong internal coherence between needs, objectives, expected results, activities, and indicators. Applicants are strongly encouraged to define one clear specific objective, include a solid monitoring plan, and describe genuine, balanced partnerships among consortium members.

The call also includes mandatory cross-cutting priorities. All proposals must integrate meaningful youth engagement, a human rights-based approach, gender equality and women’s empowerment, and conflict sensitivity throughout the design and implementation of the action.

How to Apply Through PROSPECT and PADOR

This is a restricted call for proposals, which means the first stage requires the submission of a concept note only. Applicants whose concept notes are pre-selected will later be invited to submit a full application.

Applications must be submitted online via PROSPECT, and organisations must register in PADOR. In addition, lead applicants and co-applicants must register in the European Commission’s Participant Register. The guidelines strongly recommend completing these registration steps well in advance rather than waiting until the final days before submission.

Applicants must submit their concept notes in English, follow the official application format carefully, and ensure that the concept note contains all relevant information because no extra annexes are evaluated at that first stage. The full application stage, for those invited, will require more detailed documentation, including the budget, logical framework, and supporting eligibility documents.

APPLY HERE. 

For more information about this apportunity, click here. 

Discover more grant opportunities, click here. 

Deadline and Key Dates

The deadline for submission of concept notes is 13 May 2026 at 15:30 Brussels time. This is the most important immediate deadline for interested applicants.

There will also be an information session on 17 April 2026 in hybrid format. Organisations interested in attending must send an email by 8 April 2026 with the requested participant details.

According to the indicative timetable, applicants can expect the following milestones:

  • information to pre-selected applicants and invitations to submit full applications: 25 June 2026
  • deadline for full applications: 11 August 2026 at 15:30 Brussels time
  • notification of contracting authority decision: 20 October 2026
  • indicative contract signature: 20 November 2026

Final Tips Before You Apply

For organisations considering this opportunity, early preparation will be essential. Because the call requires consortium formation, registration on multiple EU platforms, and a strong concept note aligned with one of the two lots, applicants should begin preparing as soon as possible.

It is especially important to select the correct lot from the beginning, build a proposal with a strong and coherent intervention logic, and make sure the consortium’s experience clearly matches the requirements set out in the guidelines. For organisations applying under Lot 1, the design of the FSTP component will be particularly important. For Lot 2, applicants must be precise in linking their action to actual Global Gateway project locations in Ethiopia.

For civil society organisations with the right structure, partnerships, and implementation capacity, this call represents a major opportunity to secure substantial EU support for long-term impact in Ethiopia.

The post EU Call for Proposals in Ethiopia 2026: Funding for Civil Society and Global Gateway Action appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Advance Your Academic Career with Leeds University Business School Post Doctoral Fellowships
item.dc:creator: Maria Ramirez
item.description:
Leeds University Business School (LUBS) is proud to announce its Post Doctoral Fellowship Scheme, designed to accelerate the careers of early-stage researchers. This two-year fellowship provides a structured and supportive environment, enabling scholars to build world-class research portfolios and gain valuable academic experience. The fellowships are available across multiple disciplines including Accounting & Finance, Analytics, […]

The post Advance Your Academic Career with Leeds University Business School Post Doctoral Fellowships appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Leeds University Business School (LUBS) is proud to announce its Post Doctoral Fellowship Scheme, designed to accelerate the careers of early-stage researchers. This two-year fellowship provides a structured and supportive environment, enabling scholars to build world-class research portfolios and gain valuable academic experience.

The fellowships are available across multiple disciplines including Accounting & Finance, Analytics, Technology & Operations, Economics, International Business, Management & Organisations, Marketing, and People, Work & Employment Relations. This initiative reflects LUBS’s commitment to strengthening research capacity and fostering impactful scholarship.

Fellowship Benefits

Fellows at LUBS receive:

  • Structured training programs tailored for early-career researchers
  • Dedicated mentoring on publications, funding applications, and career development
  • Opportunities for teaching experience across a variety of disciplines
  • Access to a supportive research community and cohort network

Additional benefits include excellent annual leave entitlement, generous pension schemes, family-friendly policies, fitness and wellbeing facilities, and extensive professional development opportunities.

Eligibility Requirements

  • PhD completed or near completion in a relevant field
  • Research experience through journals, conferences, or similar platforms, with strong communication skills
  • Teaching experience and ability to work independently and collaboratively
  • Strong qualitative or quantitative methodological skills with advanced research training
  • Commitment to ongoing professional development

Application Process

Applicants must engage with a potential mentor who is a current faculty member in the relevant department to discuss research plans and secure a supporting statement. This step is essential for a successful application.

Interested candidates can apply via the following links:

  • Accounting & Finance Department
  • Analytics, Technology and Operations Department
  • Economics Department
  • International Business Department
  • Marketing Department
  • Management and Organisation Department
  • People, Work and Employment Department

APPLY HERE. 

For more information about this apportunity, click here. 

Discover more fellowship opportunities, click here. 

Important Dates

Release Date: 31 March 2026
Closing Date: 31 May 2026

For international applicants, eligibility under the Skilled Worker visa or Global Talent visa may apply. Further details can be found on UK Government Skilled Worker visa and Global Talent visa pages.

 

The post Advance Your Academic Career with Leeds University Business School Post Doctoral Fellowships appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Launch Your Career in AI and Data for Global Impact: Dalberg Data Insights AI Engineering Fellowship 2026
item.dc:creator: Maria Ramirez
item.description:
Are you a recent graduate passionate about Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Data Science? Do you aspire to use your technical skills to tackle global challenges and drive social impact? The Dalberg Data Insights AI Engineering Fellowship 2026 is your gateway to hands-on experience in creating innovative data solutions for real-world development problems. About Dalberg Data […]

The post Launch Your Career in AI and Data for Global Impact: Dalberg Data Insights AI Engineering Fellowship 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Are you a recent graduate passionate about Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Data Science? Do you aspire to use your technical skills to tackle global challenges and drive social impact? The Dalberg Data Insights AI Engineering Fellowship 2026 is your gateway to hands-on experience in creating innovative data solutions for real-world development problems.

About Dalberg Data Insights

Dalberg Data Insights (DDI) bridges the AI adoption gap in Low- and Middle-Income Countries (LMICs), ensuring no population is left behind in the digital transformation. As part of the global Dalberg Group, DDI designs AI strategies, develops solutions, provides AI literacy training, and pilots responsible AI initiatives for impactful organizations. Their proprietary AI platform, augment.impact, enables the creation of advanced AI analysts and advisors (aia2), helping clients efficiently transform data into actionable insights.

The AI Engineering Fellowship Program

The 6-month fellowship is designed to develop future leaders in AI and data science. Selected fellows will:

  • Rotate through roles including data engineer, data scientist, and Gen AI specialist
  • Contribute to building data pipelines, AI solutions, and visualization tools
  • Participate in code reviews and improve technical processes
  • Drive initiatives to enhance workflows and implement innovative solutions

While full-time employment is not guaranteed, high-performing fellows may be offered permanent positions within Dalberg.

Why Join the Fellowship?

The program offers unmatched professional and technical development:

  • Technical Mastery: Build advanced skills in AI, data engineering, and data science
  • Professional Growth: Understand project responsibilities, explore your strengths, and align with organizational needs
  • Impactful Work: Contribute to AI-driven solutions for global challenges such as pandemics, climate change, and youth unemployment

Who Should Apply

Ideal candidates are curious, resourceful, and self-driven. You should:

  • Have a strong background in AI, Data Science, or related fields
  • Be proficient in Python and other programming languages
  • Thrive in multicultural teams and fast-paced environments
  • Be authorized to work in Kenya

Key Dates

Application Deadline: April 17, 2026
Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, so early submission is encouraged.

Join Dalberg Data Insights

If you’re ready to launch your career in AI and data for social impact, apply for the AI Engineering Fellowship 2026 in Nairobi, Kenya. Transform your skills into solutions that drive global change.

APPLY HERE. 

For more information about this apportunity, click here. 

Discover more fellowship opportunities, click here. 

The post Launch Your Career in AI and Data for Global Impact: Dalberg Data Insights AI Engineering Fellowship 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: UNODC Junior Professional Officer (JPO) in Crime Research – Work on Global Security from Dakar
item.dc:creator: Maria Ramirez
item.description:
The United Nations Office on Drugs and Crime is offering an exceptional opportunity for young professionals to launch an impactful international career. Through the United Nations Junior Professional Officer Programme, aspiring researchers can contribute to cutting-edge work addressing organized crime, terrorism, and drug-related challenges in West and Central Africa. 📌 About the Opportunity This Junior […]

The post UNODC Junior Professional Officer (JPO) in Crime Research – Work on Global Security from Dakar appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The United Nations Office on Drugs and Crime is offering an exceptional opportunity for young professionals to launch an impactful international career. Through the United Nations Junior Professional Officer Programme, aspiring researchers can contribute to cutting-edge work addressing organized crime, terrorism, and drug-related challenges in West and Central Africa.

📌 About the Opportunity

This Junior Professional Officer (JPO) position is based in Dakar, Senegal, within UNODC’s Regional Office for West and Central Africa. It is designed for early-career researchers passionate about data, policy, and international development.

In this role, you will work at the intersection of research and policy, supporting evidence-based strategies to combat transnational crime and strengthen justice systems across the region.

🎯 Key Responsibilities

As a JPO in Crime Research, you will:

  • Conduct research on transnational organized crime, terrorism, and drug trafficking
  • Analyze complex datasets to generate actionable insights
  • Contribute to high-level UNODC regional and global reports
  • Support capacity-building initiatives in Member States
  • Participate in field missions across West and Central Africa

🎓 Eligibility Criteria

To qualify for this role, applicants must meet the following requirements:

  • Be 32 years old or younger by 31 December 2026
  • Hold an advanced degree in:
    • Statistics
    • Sociology
    • Criminology
    • Economics
    • Or related social sciences
  • Have:
    • At least 2 years of research experience, OR
    • 4 years of experience with a bachelor’s degree
  • Demonstrate fluency in both English and French
  • Be a national of an eligible country (see full list in official job description)

🌍 Why This Opportunity Matters

This role offers more than just a job—it’s a gateway to a meaningful international career:

  • 🌐 Work with a leading UN agency tackling global crime issues
  • 📊 Gain hands-on experience in high-impact research and data analysis
  • ✈️ Participate in international missions and fieldwork
  • 🤝 Collaborate with global experts and policymakers
  • 🚀 Build a strong foundation for future UN or international roles

📝 Application Process

The JPO recruitment process is managed through donor countries participating in the UN JPO Programme. Here’s how it works:

  1. Donor countries publish available JPO vacancies
  2. Candidates apply through their respective national JPO portals
  3. Shortlisted applicants are reviewed and interviewed by UN offices
  4. Final selection is made by the hosting UN entity

Because of this structure, candidates should regularly check their country’s official JPO programme website or Ministry of Foreign Affairs.

⏳ Application Deadline

26 April 2026 – 6:00 PM (EST)

Make sure to apply before the deadline through the appropriate national JPO channel.

APPLY HERE. 

For more information about this apportunity, click here. 

Discover more job opportunities, click here. 

🚀 Start Your Global Career

With over 350 JPOs currently serving across more than 50 UN departments worldwide, this programme offers a proven pathway into the United Nations system.

If you are passionate about research, global security, and making a tangible impact, this opportunity with UNODC could be your next big step.

The post UNODC Junior Professional Officer (JPO) in Crime Research – Work on Global Security from Dakar appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Africa IFI Community Grants 2026: Up to £1,500 Funding for Education Projects Across Africa
item.dc:creator: Maria Ramirez
item.description:
Applications are now open for the Africa IFI Community Grants 2026, an initiative designed to support community-based organizations across Africa that are improving education outcomes for children and young people. This grant program offers funding of up to £1,500 GBP per project, aiming to ensure fair, transparent, and equitable distribution of financial support to grassroots […]

The post Africa IFI Community Grants 2026: Up to £1,500 Funding for Education Projects Across Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the Africa IFI Community Grants 2026, an initiative designed to support community-based organizations across Africa that are improving education outcomes for children and young people.

This grant program offers funding of up to £1,500 GBP per project, aiming to ensure fair, transparent, and equitable distribution of financial support to grassroots initiatives. The number of grants awarded will depend on the availability of funds in each round.

Grant Timeline

The application cycle for the Africa IFI Community Grants 2026 is as follows:

  • Applications Open: 30 March 2026
  • Applications Close: 13 April 2026
  • Successful Applicants Notified: To Be Determined (approximately 1–2 months after closing)

About the Grant Program

The Africa IFI grant portfolio is committed to empowering locally driven organizations that are making a direct impact within their communities. The program prioritizes projects that focus on improving access to quality education for children and youth up to the age of 25.

By supporting grassroots organizations, the initiative aims to strengthen community-led solutions and promote long-term educational development across the continent.

Funding Details

  • Grant amount: Up to £1,500 GBP
  • Coverage: Projects across African countries
  • Allocation: Based on funding availability per round

Eligibility Criteria

To qualify for this grant, applicants must meet the following requirements:

  • The organization must be operating within an African country
  • The proposed project must focus on improving education for children or young adults up to age 25
  • The organization must have been operational for at least 12 months
  • The organization must be community-based

Responsibilities of Successful Applicants

Organizations selected for funding will be required to:

  • Implement the proposed project within the agreed timeframe
  • Submit a detailed report within 3 months after project completion
  • Clearly outline project outcomes and how funds were utilized

Important Notes

  • Applications submitted after the deadline will not be considered
  • Incomplete applications will be automatically disqualified
  • Applicants are encouraged to carefully review all requirements before submission

Deadline

All applications must be submitted by 13 April 2026. Early applications are strongly encouraged to avoid disqualification due to last-minute errors.

APPLY HERE. 

For more information about this apportunity, click here. 

Discover more grant opportunities, click here. 

The post Africa IFI Community Grants 2026: Up to £1,500 Funding for Education Projects Across Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Transform Your Philanthropic Impact: Join the AVPN Philanthropy Fellowship 2026
item.dc:creator: Maria Ramirez
item.description:
Introduction If you’re ready to take your philanthropic work to the next level and collaborate with influential leaders across Asia, the AVPN Philanthropy Fellowship 2026 offers a unique opportunity to gain world-class insights, build strategic partnerships, and drive measurable impact. Delivered by AVPN Academy in partnership with the Cambridge Judge Business School, this hybrid fellowship […]

The post Transform Your Philanthropic Impact: Join the AVPN Philanthropy Fellowship 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Introduction

If you’re ready to take your philanthropic work to the next level and collaborate with influential leaders across Asia, the AVPN Philanthropy Fellowship 2026 offers a unique opportunity to gain world-class insights, build strategic partnerships, and drive measurable impact. Delivered by AVPN Academy in partnership with the Cambridge Judge Business School, this hybrid fellowship blends virtual learning with an immersive, in-person experience in New Delhi.

Programme Overview

The AVPN Philanthropy Fellowship is designed to equip professionals and changemakers with the tools needed to deploy philanthropic capital effectively for sustainable and scalable outcomes. The programme focuses on aligning philanthropic efforts with global development priorities, including the UN Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs).

Participants will explore real-world case studies, learn strategic frameworks, and engage with a global network of peers committed to advancing impactful philanthropy.

Key Programme Dates

  • 16 April 2026 – Virtual induction and kickoff
  • 20–23 April 2026 – Virtual learning sessions
  • 24–27 August 2026 – In-person convening in New Delhi

Application Deadline: 3 April 2026 (or until seats are filled)

What You’ll Learn

The fellowship covers a wide range of essential themes, including:

  • Strategic deployment of philanthropic capital
  • Building and managing multi-stakeholder partnerships
  • Scaling impact through sustainable models
  • Governance and leadership in philanthropy
  • Measuring and evaluating long-term impact

Participants will gain insights into transitioning from traditional to strategic philanthropy, leveraging branding for greater influence, and aligning initiatives with national and global development frameworks.

Programme Benefits

  • Access to expert-led workshops and executive education
  • Certificate of attendance
  • Networking with global leaders and peers
  • Complimentary ticket to the AVPN Global Conference 2026
  • Hands-on learning through real-world case studies
  • Leadership and strategic thinking development

Programme Format

This is a hybrid programme combining:

  • Virtual Learning: Interactive modules delivered over four days
  • In-Person Experience: A four-day convening alongside the AVPN Global Conference in New Delhi

Programme Fees

  • USD 2,000 for AVPN members
  • USD 3,000 for non-members
  • Complimentary for AVPN Suite Pass subscribers

Note: Travel and accommodation costs for the in-person convening are not included.

Who Should Apply

This fellowship is ideal for individuals and professionals who are actively involved in or transitioning into the philanthropic sector, including:

  • Philanthropy practitioners and advisors
  • Corporate professionals driving social impact
  • Policymakers and government officials
  • Academics and researchers
  • Individual philanthropists and foundation leaders

Why You Should Apply

The AVPN Philanthropy Fellowship is more than a learning programme—it is a gateway to becoming part of a transformative community shaping the future of giving in Asia and beyond. With limited seats available, this is a competitive and high-impact opportunity for those serious about driving change.

How to Apply

Interested candidates should apply as soon as possible, as only shortlisted applicants will be notified and spaces are limited.

APPLY HERE. 

For more information about this apportunity, click here. 

Discover more fellowship opportunities, click here. 

The post Transform Your Philanthropic Impact: Join the AVPN Philanthropy Fellowship 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Toronto Urban Fellows Program 2026–2027: Paid Opportunity to Work on Strategic City Projects in Canada
item.dc:creator: Maria Ramirez
item.description:
The Toronto Urban Fellows Research Associate role (Job ID: 62752) is now open, offering a prestigious opportunity to work directly with the City of Toronto on impactful policy, research, and urban development projects. This full-time, 12-month paid position is part of the renowned Toronto Urban Fellows Program and is designed for recent graduates eager to […]

The post Toronto Urban Fellows Program 2026–2027: Paid Opportunity to Work on Strategic City Projects in Canada appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Toronto Urban Fellows Research Associate role (Job ID: 62752) is now open, offering a prestigious opportunity to work directly with the City of Toronto on impactful policy, research, and urban development projects.

This full-time, 12-month paid position is part of the renowned Toronto Urban Fellows Program and is designed for recent graduates eager to contribute to one of North America’s most dynamic cities.

Why Toronto?

Toronto is Canada’s largest city and a global hub for business, finance, arts, and culture. With a population of approximately 2.8 million people, it consistently ranks among the most livable cities in the world.

Joining the Toronto Public Service means becoming part of an innovative and diverse workforce committed to shaping the future of urban living.

Position Overview

  • Role: Toronto Urban Fellow – Research Associate
  • Duration: 12 months (Full-time)
  • Salary: $81,593.24 annually
  • Working Hours: 35 hours/week (Monday to Friday)
  • Number of Positions: 17
  • Location: Various divisions across the City of Toronto

Application Deadline: April 13, 2026

Job Summary

As a Research Associate, you will support policy development, research, and strategic projects focused on social, economic, and urban issues. You will work alongside senior professionals to deliver meaningful insights that influence decision-making across the city.

Key Responsibilities

  • Conduct policy research and data analysis
  • Support project design and implementation
  • Analyze qualitative and quantitative data
  • Prepare reports, briefing notes, and presentations
  • Engage in stakeholder consultations and public engagement
  • Evaluate government policies and legislative proposals
  • Contribute to equity, diversity, and inclusion initiatives

Key Qualifications

To be considered, applicants must:

  • Hold a Master’s, PhD, JD, or LLB (completed within the past 3 years, not earlier than January 2023)
  • Have at least one year of relevant work experience in policy, research, or related fields
  • Demonstrate strong skills in:
    • Research and analysis
    • Project management
    • Communication and stakeholder engagement
  • Be legally eligible to work in Canada

Additional assets (not mandatory):
Experience in Finance, Accounting, or Information Technology

Cover Letter Requirements

Applicants must submit a maximum 500-word cover letter structured into three sections:

  1. My Contribution and Expectations
    • What you will bring and hope to gain
  2. My Interest
    • Your engagement with urban issues and public service
  3. Significant Achievements
    • Academic, professional, or community accomplishments

Recruitment Process

  • Resume and cover letter screening
  • Written assessment (shortlisted candidates)
  • Interview stage (final selection)

Equity and Inclusion Commitment

The City of Toronto is committed to building a diverse and inclusive workforce. Applicants from Indigenous communities and equity-deserving groups are strongly encouraged to apply and share how their lived experiences shape their work.

Information Session

📅 April 7, 2026 at 1:00 PM
🔗 https://events.teams.microsoft.com/event/ca64fc11-7bdd-4402-b8e8-9efc9d64e8c8@f0bc8ec6-9ed8-4d0c-9189-411ad949cc65

Additional Notes

  • Current and former City of Toronto employees are not eligible
  • Successful candidates may require:
    • Police Reference Check
    • TB/Immunization clearance

How to Apply

Prepare your resume and a compelling cover letter that highlights your qualifications, experience, and passion for public service.

Apply before the April 13, 2026 deadline to secure your chance at this competitive opportunity.

APPLY HERE. 

For more information about this apportunity, click here. 

Discover more internship opportunities, click here. 

The post Toronto Urban Fellows Program 2026–2027: Paid Opportunity to Work on Strategic City Projects in Canada appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: ASPPH/CDC Tribal Health Department Fellowship 2026–2027: Paid Public Health Opportunity in Tribal Communities
item.dc:creator: Maria Ramirez
item.description:
The ASPPH/CDC Tribal Health Department Fellowship Program 2026–2027 is a unique, fully funded opportunity for public health graduates passionate about making a meaningful impact in Tribal communities. This one-year, paid fellowship places participants directly within Tribal Health Departments, where they collaborate with experienced Tribal health leaders and contribute to real-world public health initiatives. With a […]

The post ASPPH/CDC Tribal Health Department Fellowship 2026–2027: Paid Public Health Opportunity in Tribal Communities appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The ASPPH/CDC Tribal Health Department Fellowship Program 2026–2027 is a unique, fully funded opportunity for public health graduates passionate about making a meaningful impact in Tribal communities. This one-year, paid fellowship places participants directly within Tribal Health Departments, where they collaborate with experienced Tribal health leaders and contribute to real-world public health initiatives.

With a strong focus on capacity building, mentorship, and culturally responsive training, this fellowship is an ideal launchpad for professionals seeking a career in Tribal public health.

Application Deadline

April 30, 2026 (11:59 PM Pacific Time)

Program Overview

Launched in 2025, the ASPPH/CDC Tribal Health Department Fellowship Program is a collaboration between the ASPPH Center for Public Health Workforce Development and the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC).

The program is designed to strengthen Tribal Health Departments by placing skilled public health graduates into host sites where they can contribute to priority-driven projects while gaining hands-on experience.

Fellows will:

  • Work on real public health challenges defined by Tribal Health Departments
  • Receive mentorship from experienced Tribal public health professionals
  • Participate in structured professional development sessions
  • Build practical, career-ready skills in Tribal public health

By the end of the fellowship, participants will have developed strong competencies and made measurable contributions to Tribal health systems.

Key Benefits of the Fellowship

Hands-On Experience

Gain practical, field-based experience that goes beyond academic learning by working directly within Tribal communities.

Expert Mentorship

Each fellow is paired with a Tribal public health mentor who provides guidance and professional support throughout the program.

Real Public Health Impact

Contribute to meaningful projects that directly improve health outcomes in Tribal populations.

Culturally Responsive Training

Engage in specialized workshops focused on Tribal public health, cultural awareness, and core competencies.

Financial Support

  • Annual stipend: $50,000
  • Additional allowance: Covers health insurance, training, and travel-related expenses

Fellowship Structure

  • Duration: September 1, 2026 – July 31, 2027
  • Commitment: Full-time
  • Extension: Possible for up to one additional year (based on performance and funding)
  • Work Mode: Primarily in-person (some hybrid/remote options available depending on placement)
  • Relocation Costs: Not covered

Fellowship Placement Locations

Participants can apply to up to three host sites. Available locations include:

  • Norman, Oklahoma
  • Pueblo of Jemez, New Mexico
  • Pembroke, North Carolina
  • Box Elder, Montana
  • Siletz, Oregon
  • Winnebago, Nebraska

Each site offers a unique project aligned with its public health priorities.

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must meet all of the following requirements:

  • Hold a graduate-level degree in public health from a CEPH-accredited ASPPH member institution
  • Have completed their degree between May 2021 and August 2026
  • Be a U.S. citizen or permanent resident (Green Card holder)

Application Process

Applications must be submitted through the ASPPH Applied Learning & Training Portal.

Required Documents

  • Resume
  • Two letters of recommendation (official letterhead)
  • One essay per selected fellowship placement
  • Proof of graduation date
  • Academic transcript (official or unofficial)

Applicants may edit and resubmit their applications at any time before the deadline.

Application Timeline

  • March 25, 2026: Applications open
  • April 30, 2026: Application deadline
  • May 1–5, 2026: Eligibility review
  • May 6–14, 2026: Host site review
  • May 18–27, 2026: Interviews
  • May 28 – June 12, 2026: Final selections

APPLY HERE. 

For more information about this apportunity, click here. 

Discover more fellowship opportunities, click here. 

Why You Should Apply

If you are passionate about advancing health equity and working closely with Tribal communities, this fellowship provides unmatched exposure, mentorship, and professional growth.

It is more than a training program—it is an opportunity to contribute to impactful public health solutions while building a meaningful career.

The post ASPPH/CDC Tribal Health Department Fellowship 2026–2027: Paid Public Health Opportunity in Tribal Communities appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: PLAC NG 2026 Legislative Internship Programme: A Gateway to Nigeria’s Legislative Process
item.dc:creator: Maria Ramirez
item.description:
The Policy and Legal Advocacy Centre (PLAC), with support from the European Union, has officially opened applications for the highly sought-after PLAC NG 2026 Legislative Internship Programme. This prestigious 10-week full-time internship offers young Nigerians a unique opportunity to gain firsthand experience in legislative practice and policymaking at the National Assembly. Designed for individuals passionate […]

The post PLAC NG 2026 Legislative Internship Programme: A Gateway to Nigeria’s Legislative Process appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Policy and Legal Advocacy Centre (PLAC), with support from the European Union, has officially opened applications for the highly sought-after PLAC NG 2026 Legislative Internship Programme. This prestigious 10-week full-time internship offers young Nigerians a unique opportunity to gain firsthand experience in legislative practice and policymaking at the National Assembly.

Designed for individuals passionate about governance, public policy, and democratic development, this programme equips participants with practical knowledge and exposure to Nigeria’s legislative system.

About the Internship Programme

The PLAC Legislative Internship Programme is an intensive learning experience that places interns directly within the National Assembly environment. Participants will engage with legislative processes, policy analysis, and governance frameworks while working alongside professionals in the field.

  • Location: Abuja, Federal Capital Territory (FCT)
  • Duration: May 2026 – July 2026 (10 weeks, full-time)

Internship Benefits

Participants in the programme will:

  • Gain hands-on experience in legislative procedures and policymaking
  • Build valuable professional networks within government institutions
  • Develop critical skills in research, communication, and public policy
  • Enhance their career prospects in governance, law, and advocacy

Although the internship is unpaid, selected candidates will receive stipends to cover transportation and lunch expenses within Abuja.

Eligibility Requirements

To qualify for the programme, applicants must:

  • Be a Nigerian citizen aged between 21 and 35 years
  • Hold at least a first degree in any discipline from a recognized tertiary institution
  • Have completed the National Youth Service Corps (NYSC) Programme
  • Demonstrate strong English communication skills (written and spoken)
  • Possess good computer skills
  • Exhibit excellent interpersonal abilities

Important Logistics Information

Applicants should note:

  • The internship is based in Abuja, and accommodation is not provided
  • Interns must be able to reside in Abuja or cover their own living expenses
  • Only transportation and lunch stipends will be provided

Application Process

Interested candidates should follow these steps carefully:

  1. Visit the official application portal
  2. Complete the online application form
  3. Upload a combined document (CV + Statement of Purpose):
    • CV (maximum 2 pages) must include:
      • State of origin
      • Date of birth
      • NYSC service details
    • Statement of Purpose (maximum 300 words) explaining your motivation
    • Total document length must not exceed 3 pages
  4. Copy and paste your Statement of Purpose into the application form

APPLY HERE. 

For more information about this apportunity, click here. 

Discover more internship opportunities, click here. 

Application Deadline

All applications must be submitted by 11:59 PM (West Africa Time – WAT), Friday, 10 April 2026.

Late or incomplete applications will not be considered.

Additional Information

  • Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted for interviews
  • The programme promotes equal opportunity participation
  • Persons with disabilities (PWDs) are strongly encouraged to apply

For inquiries, contact: internship2026@placng.org

This internship represents a powerful stepping stone for young Nigerians eager to contribute to national development through policy and legislative engagement.

The post PLAC NG 2026 Legislative Internship Programme: A Gateway to Nigeria’s Legislative Process appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
